description: LocalWiki DISCRIMINATING chunk-level fixture — KI (test DB). Expected tokens are exact chunk_ids, not source files. The semantic slice is deliberately hard: each query paraphrases a specific chunk's fact with vocabulary ABSENT from that chunk, so the lexical arm misses it in the top-10 (recall@10 headroom) while the dense arm can still find it. Authored chunk-first and every label verified against the live index (lexical rank vs semantic rank) — see the exact/topical slice for lexical-favouring controls that hybrid must not regress.
status: DRAFT — hand-verified on the live KI index (bge-m3). semantic cases: lexical rank >10 AND semantic rank <=3, relevance confirmed by reading the chunk. exact/topical: lexical rank 1. This fixture exists to give recall@10 the headroom the source-level fixtures lack, so idea.md's kill-criterion is measurable.

queries.query: what graphics cards were used and how long did training take for the base transformer
how long does a signal take to travel to orbit and back and why that hurts interactivity
what makes launching hardware into space so expensive per kilogram
which open model can run on a laptop with an apple chip
keeping company data in-house on your own servers rather than a public cloud
the requirement that the whole solution must run inside the company premises with backups
Falcon 9 launch cost per kilogram to low earth orbit
positional encoding sine and cosine functions
